content: We already have the right storage record: product state and queue runtime state should stay separate. Tighten it, do not fork it. The real rule is about different lifecycle and recovery semantics, not just 'two sqlite files'.
Understood. This is the same durable decision with better wording, not a new decision.
Please avoid duplicate records. Update the existing rule so future sessions understand why the boundary exists.
Right. The durable point stays the same: separate product-domain state from queue-processing state, because they fail and recover differently.
